disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Hyperkeratotic plantar lesion with DISRUPTED skin lines and pinpoint black dots (thrombosed capillaries); pain on direct vertical pressure
trigger_question_examples: Do the skin lines stop/interrupt across the lump, and are there tiny black dots in it? Is the pain worst on direct downward pressure?
Is it more painful pressing straight down than squeezing the sides?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, the skin lines are interrupted across it, there are tiny black dots, and it hurts most pressing straight down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No surrounding hot/red swelling, no thick pus, no fever, and no continuous throbbing at rest (deep-infection/abscess screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any hot/red swelling, thick pus, fever, or throbbing pain even when resting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No hot swelling, no pus, no fever, and no pain at rest — only when I stand on it.
